---
name: Methodology change proposal
about: Propose a change to the skill's methodology (evidence tags, frontier-scan gate, candidate discipline, anti-AI-tells, etc.). Open this BEFORE a PR.
title: "[Methodology] "
labels: methodology
---

<!--
The methodology is opinionated by design. Changes need a reason beyond
preference. Open this issue first; if the change has consensus, then
open the PR. PRs against the methodology without a prior issue may be
asked to roll back.
-->

**What you want to change**

<!-- Specific section. Name the file and heading (e.g., `templates/CLAUDE.md.tmpl` § "Frontier-scan gate"). -->

**Current behavior**

<!-- How it works today. Quote the relevant text if it helps. -->

**Proposed change**

<!-- How you want it to work. Concrete wording is welcome. -->

**Why**

<!--
The case for the change. Concrete reasons land better than abstract
ones — a real failure mode you've encountered, a contradicting source,
a class of users the current behavior excludes, an audit finding from
running the skill on a real case.
-->

**What this affects**

<!--
Which templates / docs / fixtures need to change. Mentally walk the
template tree: does this touch CLAUDE.md.tmpl, standards.md.tmpl,
research-methodology.md.tmpl, frontier-scan-status.md.tmpl, the SKILL.md
generation logic, persona fixtures, validate.sh checks?
-->

**What stays the same**

<!--
Helps reviewers see the seam — anything you considered changing but
deliberately left alone.
-->
